flashcards.back: A coordination mechanism that ensures only one process accesses a shared resource at a time in distributed systems.

flashcards.front: Name the three essential properties of mutual exclusion algorithms.
flashcards.back: Safety (only one process in CS), Liveness (no deadlock), and Fairness (no starvation).

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of the centralized algorithm?
flashcards.back: Simple implementation with only 3 messages per critical section entry.

flashcards.front: What is the main disadvantage of the centralized algorithm?
flashcards.back: Single point of failure - if the coordinator crashes, the system is blocked.

flashcards.front: How does Lamport's algorithm use logical clocks?
flashcards.back: It uses timestamped requests to establish a global ordering of critical section requests.
